It belongs to the genus Oxypetalum, which includes several other species of plants. Oxypetalum hoehnei is native to Brazil and is part of the order Gentianales. The plant is further classified into the class Magnoliopsida, which includes flowering plants with two cotyledons. intro: Oxypetalum hoehnei, also known as "Brazilian love plant," is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family, characterized by its unique, heart-shaped flowers. Traditionally, Oxypetalum hoehnei has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including anxiety, insomnia, and digestive problems, due to its reported anxiolytic, sedative, and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Oxypetalum hoehnei include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, such as oxypetaline, which are believed to contribute to its therapeutic effects.
